name: Raft Consensus
def: A consensus algorithm for replicating a log across nodes with an understandable leader-based design.
problem: Keep a replicated state machine consistent despite crashes — agreeing on an ordered log.
mechanism: Nodes elect a leader by majority vote; the leader appends entries and replicates them; entries commit once a majority acknowledges. Terms and election timeouts handle failures.
whenNot: AP systems favouring availability over consistency (use gossip/CRDTs)
myths: "Raft is faster than Paxos" — comparable; it optimises understandability, not speed

complexity.Note: Tolerates ⌊(N−1)/2⌋ failures; commit needs a majority
